description:
Advances: mutagen.ast

Deliverable: migrate .spec/specs/ast.spec.md to the SpecLedEx schema:
- spec-scenarios block: reshape each scenario to given:/when:/then: arrays (bespoke shape fails parse with 'expected array').
- spec-verification block: replace execute:/source_file: stubs with command: and target: entries; every requirement gets a verification entry.
- spec-meta: flip status: draft -> active once the file validates.
- keep prose, intent, id, surface, and decisions links intact.

Verification:
- mix spec.validate emits ZERO errors for mutagen.ast (baseline: 4 errors on this file).
- subject status is active in .spec/state.json.

Out of Scope (files): every .spec.md EXCEPT ast.spec.md; lib/**; test/**.
Allowed touches: .spec/specs/ast.spec.md only.
Out of Scope (intent): no realized_by bindings (phase2); no code/test edits; do not alter requirement ids or delete requirements.
Merge gate: mix spec.validate (0 errors for mutagen.ast) && mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED

Migrated .spec/specs/ast.spec.md to the SpecLedEx schema.

Changes (Allowed touches only: ast.spec.md + generated state.json):
- spec-scenarios: reshaped all 9 scenarios (s1-s9) to given:/when:/then: arrays. Quoted/block-literal scalars containing flow-collection syntax (e.g. `[line: 1]`, `[:Foo, :Bar]`) to satisfy the YAML parser.
- spec-verification: v1 and v3 converted from kind: source_file (path:/execute:) to kind: command with target: mix test <path>; v2 converted command: -> target:. All 5 requirements (r1-r5) retain a covering verification entry.
- spec-meta: status draft -> active.
- Prose, intent, ids, surface, decisions links unchanged. No requirement ids altered or deleted. No realized_by bindings added (phase2).
- Regenerated tracked .spec/state.json (committed with the edit).

Merge gate (local):
- mix spec.validate -> ZERO errors AND zero warnings for subject mutagen.ast (other unmigrated subjects still show errors; expected this phase).
- subject mutagen.ast status = active in .spec/state.json (meta.status).
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors -> clean.
- mix test -> 640 tests, 0 failures (19 excluded).

Commit b7d488e on branch mutagen-835.2.9/migrate-ast.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 0eb050fa-440a-426e-9f51-bfd4d1ee50de
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/0eb050fa-440a-426e-9f51-bfd4d1ee50de.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (.spec/specs/ast.spec.md, .spec/state.json)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 (state.json is the exempt generated index)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no other *.spec.md, no lib/**, no test/**)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
  - v1 (command: mix test test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs): exit 0, 28 tests, 0 failures
  - v2 (command grep 'defp alias_to_module|defp find_module_body' lib/mutagen_ex/): zero matches, exit 1. Exit 1 IS the pass signal here — grep returns 1 on no-match, and empty output is the spec's expected outcome (no donor duplicates remain). Not a zero-coverage failure; the command ran and exercised the lib tree.
  - v3 (command: mix test test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs): exit 0, 4 tests, 0 failures
- Requirements coverage:
  - r1 (alias_to_module/1 total contract): covered by v1, mix test ast_test.exs (28 tests). Would fail if alias_to_module returned wrong module / raised on non-atom parts — the unit tests assert exact returns and nil-on-garbage.
  - r2 (find_module_body/2 total contract): covered by v1 (28 tests, same file). Would fail if a defined module's body were not returned as {:ok, body} or a missing module did not return :not_found.
  - r3 (node_line/1 total): covered by v1 (28 tests). Would fail if a node with [line: N] meta returned wrong/nil, or a 2-tuple/literal raised instead of returning nil.
  - r4 (donor duplicates deleted): covered by v2 grep (ran over lib/mutagen_ex/, zero hits). Would fail if any donor module reintroduced a 'defp alias_to_module' or 'defp find_module_body' — grep would print a hit and exit 0.
  - r5 (donor-equivalence, should): covered by v3, mix test ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs (4 tests). Would fail if the lifted helper diverged from the preserved pre-.25 fixture output on any corpus AST.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. realized_by count = 0 (phase2 deferred). Req ids r1-r5 identical to base ref; none altered or deleted. spec-meta surface list unchanged. No lib/** or test/** edits. decisions list is [] in both base and HEAD (nothing to preserve).
- Project verification: pass — mix spec.validate reports ZERO findings (0 errors, 0 warnings) referencing mutagen.ast / ast.spec.md. The 61 total findings are all in other unmigrated subjects (mutation_enumeration, test_selection), expected this phase.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- All 9 scenarios reshaped to given/when/then arrays parse cleanly (no 'expected array' parse_error for ast.spec.md). Scenario covers fields still map s1-s3->r1, s4-s5->r2, s6-s7->r3, s8->r4, s9->r5.
- Unlike the failed test_selection sibling, the command-kind entries here run real, non-zero test counts (28 + 4) and pass; v2's exit-1 is the documented empty-match success, not a zero-coverage exit-1.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)

No auditor was spawned per selective-audit policy.
APPROVED (auto-gate). Rebased + regen state.json, ff-merged. Closing.
